events.text: The patient is a 63-year-old female named Margaret Okonkwo. She has a history of hypertension managed with amlodipine, hyperlipidemia on a statin, and a remote 20-pack-year smoking history (quit at age 48). She is of West African descent, a retired secondary school administrator, and lives with her adult daughter. Performance status ECOG 1 at initial presentation.

At age 63 years, the patient had a diagnosis of Stage IV (AJCC 8th edition; cT3bN2M1) RENAL CELL CARCINOMA, CLEAR CELL TYPE (ICD-O-3 histology code 8310/3), primary site RIGHT KIDNEY (ICD-O-3 site code C64.9). The right renal mass measured 11.4 cm with extension into the right renal vein and infrarenal inferior vena cava (cT3b). Retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy was present with multiple nodes up to 2.8 cm in the aortocaval region (N2). Bilateral pulmonary metastases were identified, the largest measuring 1.9 cm in the right lower lobe. Sarcomatoid differentiation was present in approximately 20% of the sampled tumor. Summary stage: DISTANT. IMDC risk group: Intermediate (2 factors: Karnofsky <80 at nadir during workup, hemoglobin below lower limit of normal). Relevant biomarkers: PD-L1 combined positive score (CPS) estimated high by biopsy specimen; no rhabdoid differentiation noted. Confirmation: POSITIVE HISTOLOGY. Tumor size: 114 mm. Laterality: RIGHT.

At age 63 years, the patient had a CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST (staging scan) which showed cancer. The right kidney contained an 11.4 cm heterogeneous mass with areas of central necrosis, involvement of the right renal vein and infrarenal IVC (approximately 3 cm of IVC thrombus, non-occlusive). Retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy was identified: right para-aortic nodes up to 2.8 cm, aortocaval node 2.2 cm, and right common iliac chain node 1.5 cm. Pulmonary findings: bilateral pulmonary nodules consistent with metastatic disease, right lower lobe 1.9 cm, right upper lobe 1.1 cm, left lower lobe 0.8 cm, left upper lobe 0.6 cm. No hepatic, osseous, or adrenal metastases identified. No brain metastases on dedicated MRI brain (performed separately). Metastatic sites: PULMONARY, LYMPH NODES (RETROPERITONEAL).

At age 63 years, the patient had an MRI BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT CONTRAST (staging evaluation) which showed no cancer. No intracranial metastases, leptomeningeal disease, or other intracranial pathology identified. Incidental finding of mild age-appropriate cerebral white matter changes.

At age 63 years, the patient had a pathology result from a CT-GUIDED CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY of the right renal mass (pre-operative confirmation). Histology: RENAL CELL CARCINOMA, CLEAR CELL TYPE (ccRCC). The tumor cells demonstrated clear cytoplasm, prominent vascularity, and Fuhrman/WHO-ISUP nuclear grade 3. Sarcomatoid differentiation was identified, comprising approximately 20% of the sampled specimen, with spindle cell morphology and increased mitotic activity in the sarcomatoid component. No rhabdoid differentiation. Immunohistochemistry: PAX8 positive, CA9 (carbonic anhydrase IX) positive (diffuse membranous), vimentin positive in sarcomatoid component, CK7 focally positive, CD10 positive. PD-L1 IHC (22C3 antibody): TPS 40%, CPS estimated 55 (high). Ki-67 proliferation index approximately 35% in sarcomatoid areas, 18% in conventional clear cell areas. PBRM1 expression: loss by IHC (suggestive of somatic mutation). BAP1: retained expression. Margins not applicable (needle biopsy).

At age 63 years, the patient had next-generation sequencing performed for clear cell renal cell carcinoma based on a specimen obtained from the right renal mass core biopsy. Platform: comprehensive genomic profiling (CGP), 324-gene panel. Findings: VHL p.R161* (nonsense mutation, pathogenic, allele frequency 48%, known driver in ccRCC), PBRM1 c.2977+1G>T (splice site mutation, pathogenic, allele frequency 42%, loss of function), SETD2 p.L1916Rfs*15 (frameshift, pathogenic, allele frequency 31%, SWI/SNF complex member), KDM5C p.T724M (missense, variant of uncertain significance, allele frequency 18%), CDKN2A homozygous deletion (copy number loss, high-level), chromosome 3p loss (consistent with VHL locus loss of heterozygosity), chromosome 5q gain. No KRAS, NRAS, BRAF mutations detected. No MET amplification. No FGFR fusions or alterations. No mismatch repair deficiency (pMMR); microsatellite stable (MSS). Tumor mutational burden (TMB): 6.2 mutations/megabase (intermediate-low). Genomic LOH: 18% (intermediate). No actionable drug-matched alterations beyond standard-of-care VEGFR/mTOR/immune checkpoint therapy. PD-L1 expression confirmed high by CGP-associated IHC. Germline analysis: no pathogenic germline variants in VHL, SDHA/B/C/D, FH, TSC1/2, MET, or FLCN detected.

At age 63 years, the patient had a multidisciplinary tumor board (MDT) assessment. The case was presented to the genitourinary oncology MDT. Discussion: The patient has IMDC intermediate-risk metastatic ccRCC with sarcomatoid features and high PD-L1 expression. The retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy and IVC thrombus are technically resectable. The pulmonary metastases are bilateral and numerous, precluding definitive local therapy to all metastatic sites. The MDT recommended: (1) cytoreductive nephrectomy with retroperitoneal lymph node dissection and IVC thrombectomy given good performance status and bulk of disease in the primary, (2) first-line systemic therapy with ipilimumab/nivolumab post-operatively given sarcomatoid features and high PD-L1 (per CheckMate 214 and sarcomatoid subgroup data). Cancer present. Disease: progressing (newly diagnosed metastatic).

At age 63 years, the patient underwent CYTOREDUCTIVE RIGHT RADICAL NEPHRECTOMY with retroperitoneal lymph node dissection (RPLND) and inferior vena cava thrombectomy. Operative approach: open (midline laparotomy). Findings: 11.8 cm right renal mass (slightly larger than CT estimate), IVC thrombus extending 4 cm into the infrarenal IVC (level I/II), multiple matted retroperitoneal lymph nodes. Complete resection of renal primary with negative surgical margins. IVC thrombus excised; IVC wall was uninvolved. RPLND: 14 lymph nodes harvested. Estimated blood loss: 850 mL; 2 units packed red blood cells transfused intraoperatively. No intraoperative complications. Post-operative course: admitted for 5 days. Developed mild ileus on post-operative day 2, resolved with conservative management. Discharged in good condition.

At age 63 years (post-nephrectomy), the patient had a surgical pathology result from the RIGHT RADICAL NEPHRECTOMY specimen. Final diagnosis: RENAL CELL CARCINOMA, CLEAR CELL TYPE, pT3bN2. Tumor size: 11.8 cm greatest dimension. Fuhrman/WHO-ISUP grade: 4 (sarcomatoid differentiation present, 20-25% of tumor volume). Sarcomatoid differentiation: present, estimated 22% of tumor. Rhabdoid differentiation: absent. Tumor necrosis: present (extensive, >50%). Lymphovascular invasion: present. Renal vein: involved by direct extension. IVC thrombus: Level I-II, 4.2 cm, non-occlusive; IVC wall margins negative. Adrenal gland: uninvolved, not resected with specimen. Surgical margins: negative (closest margin 2 mm, renal sinus fat). Lymph nodes: 9 of 14 lymph nodes positive for metastatic ccRCC (9/14); largest involved node 3.1 cm with extranodal extension. Sarcomatoid component also present in lymph node metastases.

At age 63 years (approximately 6 weeks post-nephrectomy, pre-systemic therapy baseline scan), the patient had a CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST. Cancer present. Metastatic disease identified: pulmonary metastases stable to slightly increased compared to staging CT: right lower lobe 2.1 cm (previously 1.9 cm), right upper lobe 1.2 cm (previously 1.1 cm), left lower lobe 0.9 cm (previously 0.8 cm), left upper lobe 0.7 cm (previously 0.6 cm). Post-surgical changes in the right retroperitoneum. No new sites of disease. No hepatic or osseous metastases. This scan establishes the new baseline for systemic therapy response assessment. Metastatic sites: PULMONARY (bilateral, 4 lesions).

At age 63 years (approximately 8 weeks post-surgery), the patient initiated FIRST-LINE systemic therapy with IPILIMUMAB 1 mg/kg IV every 3 weeks (for 4 doses) plus NIVOLUMAB 3 mg/kg IV every 3 weeks (for 4 induction doses), followed by NIVOLUMAB 480 mg IV every 4 weeks (maintenance), per CheckMate 214 protocol. Treatment intent: palliative (disease control/overall survival benefit in metastatic setting). Rationale: IMDC intermediate risk, sarcomatoid features (22%), high PD-L1 (CPS 55), prior nephrectomy completed. Premedication: acetaminophen and diphenhydramine per institutional protocol. Baseline labs: CBC within normal limits (Hgb 10.8 g/dL, mildly low, attributed to post-surgical state), CMP within normal limits, TSH 1.8 mIU/L (normal), cortisol AM 14.2 mcg/dL (normal), LFTs within normal limits.

At age 63 years (cycle 2, week 6 of ipilimumab/nivolumab), the patient had an oncologist office visit. The patient tolerated cycle 1 well. Cycle 2 administered. She reports mild fatigue (grade 1), mild nausea (grade 1, managed with ondansetron PRN), and two loose stools per day above baseline (grade 1 diarrhea). No rash, no arthralgia. ECOG PS: 1. Labs: TSH 3.2 mIU/L (normal), LFTs within normal limits, CBC with mild anemia (Hgb 10.4 g/dL). Cancer present (pre-response assessment). No immune-related adverse events requiring intervention at this time. Continue therapy as planned.

At age 63 years (cycle 3, approximately week 10 of ipilimumab/nivolumab), the patient developed IMMUNE-RELATED COLITIS (irAE), CTCAE Grade 2. She presented with 6-8 loose/watery stools per day above baseline, associated with mild lower abdominal cramping. No blood in stool. Colonoscopy performed: diffuse mild colonic mucosal edema and erythema, consistent with immune-mediated colitis; biopsies showed increased intraepithelial lymphocytes and crypt distortion. Management: ipilimumab held (nivolumab continued with caution); oral budesonide 9 mg daily initiated. Stool cultures negative. Symptoms improved to grade 1 within 10 days. Ipilimumab re-introduced at next cycle after resolution to grade 1 or less.

At age 63 years (approximately 12 weeks/3 months of ipilimumab/nivolumab, first restaging), the patient had a CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST. Cancer present. Response assessment per RECIST 1.1: PARTIAL RESPONSE. Pulmonary metastases decreased: right lower lobe 1.3 cm (from 2.1 cm, -38%), right upper lobe 0.7 cm (from 1.2 cm, -42%), left lower lobe 0.5 cm (from 0.9 cm, -44%), left upper lobe 0.4 cm (from 0.7 cm, -43%). No retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy. No new metastatic sites. Sum of diameters: 2.9 cm (from baseline 5.0 cm, -42%). This confirms partial response. Metastatic sites: PULMONARY (bilateral, 4 lesions, all decreased).

At age 63 years (after completing 4 cycles of ipilimumab induction, transitioning to nivolumab maintenance), the patient had an oncologist office visit. Completed 4 doses of ipilimumab (1 dose was held due to grade 2 colitis, completed the 4th dose with 1-cycle delay). Now transitioning to nivolumab 480 mg IV every 4 weeks (maintenance). Restaging CT shows partial response (-42%). Patient feeling better overall, fatigue improved (grade 1). Colitis now grade 1 on budesonide taper (being weaned). TSH: 0.4 mIU/L (mildly low, monitoring). Adrenal function normal. Cancer present with response to therapy. Continue nivolumab maintenance.

At age 63 years (approximately 5 months into therapy, during nivolumab maintenance), the patient developed IMMUNE-RELATED HYPOTHYROIDISM (irAE), CTCAE Grade 2. She reported increasing fatigue, cold intolerance, and mild constipation over several weeks. TSH: 18.4 mIU/L (markedly elevated), free T4: 0.6 ng/dL (low). Thyroid peroxidase antibodies: positive (high titer). Diagnosis: immune checkpoint inhibitor-associated hypothyroidism. Nivolumab continued without interruption. Levothyroxine initiated at 50 mcg daily, titrated to 75 mcg daily over subsequent weeks with normalization of TSH (follow-up TSH: 2.1 mIU/L on levothyroxine 75 mcg). Symptoms improved substantially. Levothyroxine to be continued indefinitely.

At age 63 years (approximately 6 months of systemic therapy, second restaging), the patient had a CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST. Cancer present. Response assessment per RECIST 1.1: MIXED RESPONSE. Pulmonary metastases: right lower lobe lesion increased from 1.3 cm to 2.4 cm (+85% from nadir, NEW target lesion progression); right upper lobe 0.6 cm (stable from 0.7 cm); left lower lobe 0.5 cm (stable); left upper lobe 0.4 cm (stable). NEW finding: a 1.3 cm right adrenal nodule, new since baseline, consistent with adrenal metastasis (biopsy recommended vs. follow-up). Sum of diameters of target lesions: 3.9 cm (from nadir 2.9 cm, +34%). Overall assessment: mixed response / early oligoprogression at right lower lobe lesion and possible new adrenal metastasis. Metastatic sites: PULMONARY, ADRENAL (new, right).

At age 63 years (approximately 6.5 months of systemic therapy), the patient had an MDT reassessment for mixed response on ipilimumab/nivolumab. Discussion: Patient has mixed response - most pulmonary lesions stable but right lower lobe lesion has significantly increased and a new right adrenal metastasis has appeared. This represents oligoprogression at 2 sites. Options discussed: (1) Continue nivolumab with local therapy to the right lower lobe (SBRT) and adrenal (SBRT or adrenalectomy); (2) Discontinue immunotherapy and switch to cabozantinib; (3) Continue and re-image at 8 weeks. Decision: given that only 2 sites are progressing, proceed with SBRT to the right lower lobe pulmonary lesion. Right adrenal biopsy planned to confirm metastatic disease before further intervention. Continue nivolumab for now while local therapy is arranged. Cancer present, mixed response to immunotherapy.

At age 63 years (approximately 7 months into therapy), the patient had a CT-guided core biopsy of the RIGHT ADRENAL NODULE. Histology: METASTATIC RENAL CELL CARCINOMA, CLEAR CELL TYPE, consistent with the known primary. Tumor cells demonstrate clear cytoplasm, PAX8 positive, CA9 positive, CD10 positive. Sarcomatoid features not identified in this specimen. Ki-67: 22%. This confirms adrenal metastasis from known ccRCC primary.

At age 63 years (approximately 7.5 months into therapy), the patient underwent STEREOTACTIC BODY RADIATION THERAPY (SBRT) to the RIGHT LOWER LOBE PULMONARY METASTASIS (2.4 cm). Technique: SBRT, linac-based, 4D-CT simulation with respiratory gating. Dose: 54 Gy in 3 fractions (18 Gy per fraction), delivered over 1 week. Planning target volume: 2.4 cm lesion with 0.5 cm isotropic margin. Treatment well-tolerated. No acute toxicity beyond mild fatigue. Concurrent nivolumab held for the SBRT week and resumed the following week. Radiation oncology follow-up arranged.

At age 63 years (approximately 9 months of systemic therapy, third restaging), the patient had a CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST and MRI BRAIN WITH CONTRAST. Cancer present. Overall assessment: PROGRESSIVE DISEASE per RECIST 1.1. Findings: RIGHT LOWER LOBE PULMONARY LESION: treated with SBRT, now 1.8 cm (radiation response, local control achieved). Right upper lobe lesion: increased from 0.6 cm to 1.8 cm (+200%). Left lower lobe lesion: increased from 0.5 cm to 1.4 cm (+180%). Left upper lobe lesion: increased from 0.4 cm to 0.9 cm (+125%). NEW hepatic lesion: segment VI, 1.6 cm, new since prior imaging. RIGHT ADRENAL nodule: 1.5 cm, slight increase from 1.3 cm (+15%). Sum of diameters: 7.4 cm (from baseline 5.0 cm, +48% from baseline; +155% from nadir). MRI brain: no intracranial metastases. Assessment: systemic progressive disease on nivolumab maintenance despite local SBRT to RLL lesion. Metastatic sites: PULMONARY (bilateral, multiple), HEPATIC (new), ADRENAL (right).

At age 63 years (9 months after starting ipilimumab/nivolumab), the patient had an oncologist office visit for progression assessment. Restaging imaging confirms progressive disease: multiple pulmonary lesions increased, new hepatic metastasis, adrenal progression. ECOG PS: 1. Labs: Hgb 9.8 g/dL (mild anemia), LFTs mildly elevated (ALT 48, AST 52, likely hepatic metastasis), creatinine 1.1 mg/dL (normal), eGFR 58 mL/min (normal for single kidney post-nephrectomy). Alkaline phosphatase: 124 U/L (mildly elevated). Decision: DISCONTINUE nivolumab. Initiate second-line therapy with CABOZANTINIB (VEGFR/MET/AXL inhibitor). Hepatic lesion to be monitored; not amenable to SBRT given multiple progressing systemic sites. SBRT to adrenal considered but deferred given systemic progression. Cancer present, progressing on immunotherapy.

At age 63 years (approximately 9.5 months after initial systemic therapy start), the patient initiated SECOND-LINE systemic therapy with CABOZANTINIB 60 mg orally once daily (starting dose per institutional protocol; label dose is 60 mg/day). Treatment intent: palliative (disease control). Rationale: progression on first-line ipilimumab/nivolumab; cabozantinib is standard second-line therapy after immunotherapy in ccRCC with favorable Phase 3 data (METEOR trial, CABOSUN subgroup data). Baseline creatinine: 1.1 mg/dL. Patient counseled on expected toxicities: hypertension, hand-foot syndrome (palmar-plantar erythrodysesthesia), diarrhea, fatigue, nausea, hepatotoxicity, and hypothyroidism (already on levothyroxine). Blood pressure at baseline: 138/84 mmHg (slightly elevated; amlodipine 10 mg/day already in use, dose not changed yet). Baseline TSH: 2.1 mIU/L on levothyroxine 75 mcg.

At age 63 years (approximately 3 weeks into cabozantinib therapy), the patient developed PALMAR-PLANTAR ERYTHRODYSESTHESIA (hand-foot syndrome, HFS), CTCAE Grade 2. She presented with painful erythema, swelling, and blistering on bilateral palms and soles, interfering with daily activities. She also reported 5-6 loose stools per day (Grade 2 diarrhea), with associated mild cramping but no blood. Management: cabozantinib dose reduced from 60 mg to 40 mg daily per protocol. Urea cream (40%) applied to affected areas twice daily; blister dressings for plantar lesions. Loperamide 4 mg initial dose then 2 mg after each loose stool for diarrhea management. Patient educated on foot care: soft shoes, padded insoles, avoid heat/friction. Dermatology consultation placed. Symptoms improved to grade 1 over the subsequent 2-3 weeks.

At age 63 years (approximately 6 weeks into cabozantinib 40 mg/day), the patient developed HYPERTENSION, CTCAE Grade 2. Blood pressure: 158/96 mmHg on repeated measurements. Amlodipine dose adjusted from 10 mg to maximum; lisinopril 10 mg daily added (also nephroprotective in setting of single kidney). Goal BP <140/90 mmHg. Subsequent BP: 138/88 mmHg (improved, within goal). Cabozantinib dose maintained at 40 mg. Electrolytes monitored (potassium 4.1 mEq/L, normal). Urinalysis: trace proteinuria (1+), 24-hour urine protein pending (ultimately 680 mg/24h, below threshold for dose modification).

At age 63 years (approximately 3 months of cabozantinib therapy, first restaging on new regimen), the patient had a CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST. Cancer present. Response assessment per RECIST 1.1: STABLE DISEASE (SD). Pulmonary metastases: right upper lobe 1.5 cm (from 1.8 cm, -17%); left lower lobe 1.2 cm (from 1.4 cm, -14%); left upper lobe 0.8 cm (from 0.9 cm, -11%). Right lower lobe: previously irradiated lesion 1.4 cm, stable/slight decrease. Hepatic segment VI lesion: 1.3 cm (from 1.6 cm, -19%). Right adrenal: 1.3 cm (stable from 1.5 cm, -13%). Sum of target lesion diameters: decreased from 7.4 cm (at cabozantinib initiation) to 6.2 cm (-16%), meeting criteria for stable disease (not sufficient for PR). No new metastatic sites. MRI brain not repeated (no neurologic symptoms). Assessment: stable disease on cabozantinib with minor tumor regression across all sites. Metastatic sites: PULMONARY (bilateral), HEPATIC (segment VI), ADRENAL (right).

At age 63 years (approximately 3 months after starting cabozantinib), the patient had an oncologist office visit for response assessment. Restaging CT shows stable disease with minor decreases across all metastatic sites. Patient reports improved quality of life compared to initial progression period. Current toxicities: hand-foot syndrome grade 1 (managed with urea cream and protective footwear); diarrhea 2-3 stools above baseline (grade 1, managed with dietary modification and PRN loperamide); fatigue grade 1-2. ECOG PS: 1. Labs: TSH 8.2 mIU/L (elevated on current levothyroxine dose; dose increased from 75 mcg to 100 mcg); ALT 42 U/L (improved from prior); creatinine 1.2 mg/dL (stable for single kidney). Blood pressure: 136/84 mmHg (at goal). Cabozantinib continued at 40 mg/day. Next restaging in 3 months. Cancer present; disease stable on cabozantinib.

At age 64 years (approximately 6 months of cabozantinib therapy, second restaging on new regimen), the patient had a CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST. Cancer present. Response assessment per RECIST 1.1: STABLE DISEASE (SD) - maintained. Pulmonary metastases: right upper lobe 1.4 cm (stable); left lower lobe 1.1 cm (stable); left upper lobe 0.7 cm (stable). Right lower lobe previously irradiated lesion: 1.3 cm (stable). Hepatic segment VI lesion: 1.2 cm (stable). Right adrenal: 1.2 cm (stable). Sum of target lesion diameters: 6.1 cm (essentially unchanged from prior 6.2 cm). No new lesions. No bone metastases. Assessment: continued stable disease on cabozantinib at 6-month mark from initiation. Metastatic sites: PULMONARY (bilateral), HEPATIC (segment VI), ADRENAL (right).

At age 64 years (6 months on cabozantinib, 15+ months from initial diagnosis of metastatic disease), the patient had an oncologist visit for ongoing management. She is maintaining stable disease on cabozantinib 40 mg/day. Quality of life acceptable; she participates in light daily walks and assists with her grandchildren. Toxicities: persistent grade 1 hand-foot syndrome (right heel most affected, managed with silicone heel cups and urea cream); grade 1 diarrhea (1-2 stools above baseline, managed with dietary fiber modification; loperamide used occasionally); grade 1 fatigue. No new adverse events. No dose reductions currently needed. Labs: TSH 2.8 mIU/L (therapeutic on levothyroxine 100 mcg); CBC: Hgb 10.2 g/dL (mild anemia, stable); platelets 198,000/mcL; creatinine 1.2 mg/dL; LFTs normal. Blood pressure: 134/82 mmHg (at goal). Patient agreeable to continue current therapy. Goals of care discussion initiated: patient's priorities are quality of life, maintaining independence, and being present for family events. Palliative care integrated co-management arranged. Cancer present; stable disease on cabozantinib.

At age 64 years (approximately 9 months on cabozantinib), the patient had an oncologist office visit. She continues on cabozantinib 40 mg/day with ongoing stable disease. A new complaint of mild right hip discomfort was noted; bone scan ordered to exclude osseous metastasis (result: negative for bone metastasis; mild degenerative changes at right hip consistent with osteoarthritis). Ongoing toxicities: grade 1 HFS (stable, managed with supportive care); grade 1 diarrhea (managed); grade 1 fatigue. No new immune-related events (off immunotherapy for 9 months). Continued monitoring of blood pressure and thyroid function. TSH: 2.5 mIU/L (therapeutic). Levothyroxine continued at 100 mcg/day. Cabozantinib dose maintained. Restaging CT scheduled in 3 months. Cancer present (stable disease per last restaging). Overall disease trajectory: prolonged stable disease on cabozantinib with manageable toxicity profile, consistent with treatment goals discussed with patient and palliative care team.

scenario_blurb: Clear cell renal cell carcinoma stage IV with sarcomatoid differentiation (20%) in a 63-year-old, presenting with large right renal mass, retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy, and pulmonary metastases. Undergoes cytoreductive nephrectomy. First-line ipilimumab/nivolumab with mixed response. Progression at 9 months, switched to cabozantinib with stable disease. Manages TKI-related hand-foot syndrome and diarrhea.
